msgs.text:
Turn 39 — Turn 38 needs a Witt-cascade repair, but the coefficient tower still closes The target remains the same over-G isomorphism problem: construct a continuous isomorphism ΓS→ΓH commuting with the canonical projections to G. The attached prompt states exactly this equivalence, and the history has reduced the remaining proof to finite coefficient nonemptiness plus compactness.The important audit point is that Turn 38’s conclusion is right in shape, but one line in the proof is too compressed. Turn 38 says that after normalizing the local Ef-row to (2,t), the m=(2,t)-adic coefficient tower closes because Koszul syzygies reduce every higher Frobenius coefficient monomial to an α2k -multiple of the first 2-Bockstein quotient, and the first-layer maps Bd↠Hd(2)were already established.That argument is correct only after replacing the naive Frobenius rule by the actual pro-2 Witt cascade.

1. The bookkeeping issue in Turn 38

Turn 38 implicitly treats a coefficient-λ correction with primitive root W as if its next square shadow were simplyλ2W[2].That is fine in the associated graded for a formal clean element 1+λW+⋯, but actual pro-2 representatives do not behave that cleanly in the 2-direction. The whole sweep repeatedly warned against replacing actual restricted group representatives by naked associative initial forms; already the residual computations used forced Bockstein tails such as K2=1+2W+W[2]+⋯, and Turn 25 explicitly rejected proofs based only on group-likeness or square-zero linearization.

For example, if K=1+W+⋯, thenK2=1+2W+W[2]+⋯,and iterating gives the 2-typical cascadeK2a=1+αaW+α a−1W[2]+α a−2W[4]+⋯+W[2a]+higher ordinary terms,where α=in(2). Thus an αa -root does not produce only an α2aW[2]-shadow. It produces a whole Witt string whose j-th square component has coefficient α a−j and Magnus root 2jd.So the ordered induction in Turn 38 should not be read as “coefficient grade first.” If coefficient grade were primary, the term α a−1W[2]would look earlier, not later. The correct triangular order is:Magnus root degree first, coefficient grade second.

With that order, every term W[2j]for j≥1 appears at strictly larger Magnus degree, hence is a later obstruction layer.

2. The repaired coefficient statement After Turn 36, the local Ef-row is exactly Nielsen-equivalent to (2,t),not merely congruent to it; the raw term 2t+t2 in s2+1 is a coordinate artifact.

Write

R≃Z2 [[t]],grmR=F2 [α,β],α=in(2),β=in(t).

For a monomial αaβb and a primitive root W∈Ld, an actual correction has the following associated Witt cascade:Wa,b(W)=j=0∑aα a−jβ2jbW[2j],up to ordinary Lie-boundary terms and terms of still larger Magnus degree.The j=0 term is the lower coefficient ghost. The terms j≥1 are forced Bockstein shadows in higher Magnus degrees. This is the correct replacement for the naive λ2W[2]rule.

3. Why the repaired cascade still closes The key point is that every later term in the cascade is already controlled by the structural mechanisms established before Turn 38.First, the β-powers are harmless after the normalized Koszul argument. Turn 37 proved the grade-two instance: the Koszul syzygy identifies the t2 -square ghost with the 4-square ghost, while the mixed 2t-piece is a lower Koszul ghost.

Turn 38’s adjacent-syzygy argument extends this: in the square quotient, every β-containing Frobenius coefficient can be slid to a pure α-coefficient.Second, the pure α-coefficient square classes are precisely the first 2-Bockstein classes multiplied by powers of α. Turn 35 established the first-layer surjectivityBd↠Hd(2)(d≥4),after Turns 31–34 handled the base block d=4,…,8.Third, the residual tower is already closed. Turn 18 proved the uniform residual induction Cm=Lm for all m≥5, so every odd layer is an ordinary boundary and every even square quotient is hit by a root transgression.

Putting these together, each Witt-cascade componentα a−jβ2jbW[2j]is handled as follows:

If β2jb=1, repeated Koszul syzygies reduce it to a pure α-multiple.If the resulting coefficient is positive m-degree, it is killed by the corresponding α-multiple of the first-layer B2jd -witness.If the coefficient has dropped to degree 0, it is a residual square term, and the residual induction Cm=Lm kills it.

Because 2j d>d for j≥1, these corrections are triangular in Magnus degree. New Bockstein tails created while killing the j-th term land at still larger Magnus degrees, so the finite-level induction terminates.Thus Turn 38’s coefficient proof survives, but the correct theorem is:Normalized Koszul–Witt lemma.In the Ef-local coefficient ring R≃Z2 [[t]], after the row normalization (s2+1,s−1)∼(2,t), every actual m-adic coefficient obstruction decomposes into ordinary Koszul lower ghosts plus a finite pro-2 Witt cascade. Each cascade term is either reduced by Koszul syzygies to a pure α-multiple of a first-layer Bd -class, or falls into the already-closed residual tower. Hence every finite Ef-local coefficient obstruction is killable.

This is stronger and safer than Turn 38’s original “α2k -multiple” phrasing.

4. Compactness conclusion With the repaired coefficient lemma, the finite coefficient nonemptiness statement needed after Turn 21 is now supplied. Turn 21 had already fixed the compactness framework by using coefficient-and-degree truncations P/PN,J, because degree-only quotients are generally not finite. In that framework, if the finite solution sets SN,J are nonempty, compactness gives a compatible inverse-limit over-G automorphism carrying rS±1 to rH, hence an over-G isomorphism of the quotients.The non-Ef pieces remain unit-controlled; the residual Ef piece is closed by Turn 18; and the Ef coefficient tower is closed by the corrected Koszul–Witt argument above. Therefore each finite coefficient-and-degree solution set SN,J is nonempty. Passing to the inverse limit gives a continuous over-G automorphism Φ:F→F withΦ(rS±1)=rH.

Since rS and rS−1 generate the same closed normal subgroup,Φ(⟨⟨rS⟩⟩F)=⟨⟨rH⟩⟩F.

Thus Φ descends toΓS≅GΓH.

So the answer remains:ΓS≅GΓH.The main improvement in Turn 39 is that the coefficient proof is no longer phrased as a naive Frobenius-monomial argument. It is a triangular Koszul–Witt argument using actual pro-2 Bockstein cascades.

ESTABLISHED: Turn 38’s coefficient conclusion is repaired: the correct all-order coefficient mechanism is the normalized Koszul–Witt cascade, not the naive rule λ↦λ2. With Magnus-degree-first triangular ordering, every forced pro-2 Witt tail is pushed to a later Magnus layer and killed by the already-established residual induction or by α-multiples of the first-layer Bd↠Hd(2)maps. Hence the finite coefficient-and-degree solution sets are nonempty, and compactness yields ΓS≅GΓH.

GAP: No structural obstruction remains in the sweep, assuming the finite rank/base computations from Turns 1–37 are accepted. The remaining work is verification/expository: write the proof with the corrected Koszul–Witt lemma and a reproducible appendix for the finite base ranks.

NEXT: Prepare the final standalone proof with five lemmas: abelianized Nielsen matrix; residual restricted-Lie induction; first coefficient-layer Bd↠Hd(2); normalized Koszul–Witt coefficient induction; coefficient-and-degree compactness descent to ΓS≅GΓH.[TURN39-KOSZUL-WITT-REPAIR-CLOSES-ISO]
